name: organization
description: Structural sub-brand belonging to a Tenant. Answers 'which brand does this belong to?' — a tenant running two academies (say 'Elite Sports' and 'Community Sports') under one Tenant runs them as two Organizations. Orthogonal to Region; the two meet at Branch which carries FKs to both. Every Tenant provisions exactly one `is_default=true` Organization on TenantProvisioned so single-brand tenants never special-case it. Multi-org tenants add Organizations per entitlement. Enterprise tenants may NEST Organizations via `parent_id` self-referential FK (parent must be same tenant + no cycles + max depth cap). Small tier is locked to 1 Organization no nesting; Medium up to 5 no nesting; Enterprise unlimited + nesting via the `organization_hierarchy` entitlement. Ships: Organization model, org CRUD HTTP surface, default-org seeder listener on `TenantProvisioned`, is_default enforcement, parent_id cycle detection, materialised tree_path for efficient descendant queries, and a branding-integration hook (an Organization row can pin its own Branding profile when the tenant subscribes to `organization_branding` — otherwise the tenant's default branding applies).

wraps.rationale: Organization is a first-party aggregate. No vendor packages wrapped. The materialised-path tree walker is a purpose-built primitive because Postgres RECURSIVE CTE is too expensive on the hot path of `is-descendant-of?` checks that fire in every scoped access decision.

tenant.description: Tenant-facing Organization CRUD + hierarchy management. Reads open to any tenant member; writes require owner/admin. Nesting via parent_id is gated by the `organization_hierarchy` entitlement. Every mutation invalidates tree_path caches for affected subtrees.

platform-admin.description: Platform-admin cross-tenant Organization observability. Read-only in v1 (writes stay tenant-plane so provenance is captured).
